channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: US diplomats and hostage envoy in Syria on first visit since Assad ouster
item.description: The first U.S. diplomats to visit Syria since President Bashar Assad's ouster earlier this month are in Damascus to hold talks with the country's new leadership and seek information on the whereabouts of missing American journalist Austin Tice.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democratic governors hammered Trump before the election. Now they're hoping to work with him
item.description: A handful of prominent Democratic governors are quickly adjusting their approach to President-elect Donald Trump before he takes office in January.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dems say 'hell no' to GOP plan B on government funding, debt ceiling
item.description: House Democrats are rallying in overwhelming opposition to the Republicans' second stab at a government funding bill, vowing to sink it on the House floor and force GOP leaders back to the table to negotiate a bipartisan compromise.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutor Fani Willis is removed from the Georgia election case against Trump and others
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A state appeals court on Thursday removed Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis from the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others, the latest legal victory for the president-elect in criminal cases that once threatened his career and freedom.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FAA bans drones in parts of New Jersey
item.description: The FAA warned that "deadly force" could be used against the drones if they pose an "imminent security threat.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon warns that a government shutdown will cost troops their pay over the holidays
item.description: The Pentagon is warning that Republicans’ decision to abandon a congressional spending plan will cost troops their paychecks over the holidays unless some agreement is reached before Friday’s deadline to prevent a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor And Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What a potential government shutdown could mean for San Diego
item.description: The federal government is barreling towards a partial shutdown after GOP lawmakers in Congress backed away from a deal on a short-term spending bill struck with Democrats just days before the Friday deadline.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about the debt ceiling debate as a government shutdown looms in Washington
item.description: A debate over the debt ceiling is at the center of a dispute over funding that is pushing the possibility of a federal government shutdown to the brink in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: ICE deportations catch up to Trump-era numbers in FY 2024 as Biden admin comes to a close
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) deportations caught up with Trump-era levels in FY 2024, according to new agency data released on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House says Biden will visit Pope Francis, Meloni in final international trip of his presidency
item.description: President Joe Biden will travel to Italy next month to meet with Pope Francis and top Italian officials for what is expected to be the final international trip of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Here's what happens during a partial government shutdown
item.description: During a partial government shutdown, nonessential services pause and federal workers face furloughs or delayed pay, but essential operations will continue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas AG's lawsuit sets up a red versus blue state abortion battle
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is suing a New York-based abortionist for violating Texas law by shipping abortion drugs into the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some House Republicans privately furious at Musk, Ramaswamy after government shutdown talks implode
item.description: Some House Republicans are privately fuming at the 11th-hour involvement of Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy in government funding talks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: The House has resoundingly rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s new plan to fund federal operations and suspend the debt ceiling a day before a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Donald Trump said he wants to ban trans athletes from competing. The reality is more nuanced
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s campaign promise to ‘ban’ transgender athletes struck a chord with voters. It’s uncertain, however, what exactly Trump will do once his second term begins in January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Fani Willis disqualified as prosecutor on Trump Georgia election case
item.description: A Georgia appeals court booted Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis's (D) office from the 2020 election interference case against President-elect Trump on Thursday due to her relationship with a top prosecutor on the case.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Former top aide to Mayor Adams surrenders to DA's office to face charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams surrendered to authorities Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to the administration of a mayor who has seen who is facing his own indictment and investigations into multiple aides and allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's words of opposition stop a bipartisan budget deal in its tracks with Musk's help
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump delivered a likely death blow to bipartisan congressional budget negotiations, rejecting the measure as full of giveaways to Democrats after billionaire ally Elon Musk whipped up outrage toward the bill and cheered on Republican lawmakers who announced
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ryan Routh, alleged would-be Trump assassin, faces new felony charge
item.description: Florida Attorney General Ashley Moody obtained an arrest warrant for Ryan Routh on an unrelated charge of attempted felony murder involving a six-year-old in a car accident.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson spending deal throws Speakership into question as floor vote approaches
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson’s handling of an end-of-year spending deal is throwing his grasp on the gavel into uncertain territory ahead of a critical Jan. 3 Speaker vote, as some GOP lawmakers question their support for the Louisiana Republican. Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate passes defense bill that will raise troop pay and aims to counter China's power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate passed a defense bill Wednesday that authorizes significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members, aims to counter China's growing power and boosts overall military spending to $895 billion while also stripping coverage of transgender medical treatments for chil
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Government funding plan collapses as Trump makes new demands days before shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump abruptly rejected a bipartisan plan Wednesday to prevent a Christmastime government shutdown, instead telling House Speaker Mike Johnson and Republicans to essentially renegotiate — days before a deadline when federal funding runs out.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Government funding plan collapses as Trump makes new demands
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has abruptly rejected a bipartisan plan to prevent a Christmastime government shutdown, instead telling House Speaker Mike Johnson and Republicans to essentially renegotiate — days before a deadline when federal funding runs out.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Paris Hilton-backed youth welfare bill passes House, heads to Biden's desk
item.description: Today is a day I will never forget. After years of sharing my story and advocating on Capitol Hill, the Stop Institutional Child Abuse Act has officially passed the U.S. Congress,” Hilton said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Senate passes defense bill that will raise troop pay and aims to counter China's power
item.description: The Senate has passed a defense bill that authorize significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members and boost overall military spending to $895 billion. It also strips coverage of transgender medical treatments for children of military members.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Government funding deal opens door to move Washington Commanders stadium to DC
item.description: The Washington Commanders could soon be kicking off in the heart of the nation’s capital. The government funding deal unveiled by congressional negotiators Tuesday paved the way for moving the Commanders' stadium from Maryland to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans butt heads on daylight saving time after Trump directive
item.description: Trump pledged that “The Republican Party will use its best efforts to eliminate Daylight Saving Time"
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Congress unveils funding deal with more than $100 billion in disaster aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congressional leaders have unveiled a stopgap spending bill that will keep the federal government funded through March 14 and provide more than $100 billion in emergency aid to help states and local communities recover from Hurricanes Helene and Milton and other natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New York judge says Trump is not immune from hush money conviction
item.description: A spokesperson for President-elect Trump said the case should never have been brought, called for its immediate dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News
